title: Rootless v2.0.4 podman pod create returns error
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. sudo apt install podman

2. Run podman pod create

**Describe the results you received:**
error message

**Describe the results you expected:**
pod created 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman --version             
podman version 2.0.4
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body: This issue relates to closed ticket #6119 .
Can you provide the error? This is working perfectly on my system.

Hello,
Thank you for your quick reply.

```
$ podman pod create
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
ERRO[0000] Error freeing pod lock after failed creation: no such file or directory 
Error: error adding Infra Container: error locating item named "manifest" for image with ID "80d28bedfe5dec59da9ebf8e6260224ac9008ab5c11dbbe16ee3ba3e4439ac2c": file does not exist
```

That ID is correct for the AMD64 pause image.

Can you try removing the image and re-pulling it?

Also, running the same command using root permissions works. 
Only when using rootless do I get this error.

```
$ podman images
REPOSITORY        TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
k8s.gcr.io/pause  3.2     80d28bedfe5d  5 months ago  687 kB

$ podman image rm -f 80d28bedfe5d
Error: 1 error occurred: * error reading image "80d28bedfe5dec59da9ebf8e6260224ac9008ab5c11dbbe16ee3ba3e4439ac2c" as image: error locating item named "manifest" for image with ID "80d28bedfe5dec59da9ebf8e6260224ac9008ab5c11dbbe16ee3ba3e4439ac2c": file does not exist
```

@mheon ,
After investigating further, from ```podman info``` it seems like my store.graphRoot path had the wrong permissions set.
After manually clearing out all the files in /home/USERNAME/.local/share/containers, I am now able to create pods as expected!
```
$ sudo rm -rf /home/USERNAME/.local/share/containers
$ podman pod create
e6376a9b23d03370ed68750f922d7eb00cd778070983d5d4f748e3c863b0f2b3
```

Thanks!
